bead.title: auto-route: emit explicit filter_reason for excluded same-harness tiers (no silent drop)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The routing_decision event currently omits sibling tiers under a multi-tier subscription harness entirely when they are not chosen — no row, no filter_reason. This is diagnostically harmful: silent absence is indistinguishable from "never enumerated." An excluded tier should always appear in candidates with eligible:false and an explicit filter_reason (e.g., power_missing, surface_mismatch, cost_excluded), the same pattern used today for openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 which surfaces with filter_reason:"power_missing".

ROOT CAUSE (pointers; confirm during fix)
- service_routing.go:1172-1189 candidate add loop — when a candidate is filtered after add(), the row is dropped from the emitted list instead of being marked eligible:false with a reason. The eligibility map keyed by modelID may also lose entries before emit.
- The routing_decision serializer needs to include excluded-candidate rows; verify in the event emitter (likely same package as score_components emitter).

PROPOSED FIX (direction)
Every catalog-known tier under a configured harness must produce either (a) an eligible candidate, or (b) a candidate row with eligible:false and a non-empty filter_reason. No silent drops permitted for catalog-known tiers under configured harnesses.

NON-SCOPE
- Per-tier enumeration of eligible candidates (sibling child enumerate-per-tier-candidates).
- Scoring-based tier selection (sibling child cost-aware-tier-selection).
- --model pin routing destination (sibling child model-pin-subscription-route).
- openrouter 401, DDx-side changes, catalog metadata additions.

CROSS-REF
https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6
bead.acceptance:
1. TestExcludedTierEmitsFilterReason — when sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata is artificially broken (power field removed in a test fixture), a policy=default dispatch under the claude harness produces a routing_decision.candidates row for claude/sonnet-4.6 with eligible:false and filter_reason:"power_missing" (matching the existing pattern for openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5).
2. TestNoSilentDropForCatalogKnownTier — for every catalog-known tier listed in a configured harness's SupportedModels, the routing_decision.candidates list contains exactly one row for that tier — either eligible or with a filter_reason. Asserted via a table-driven test that enumerates expected tiers and matches them against the event.
3. TestFilterReasonStringsAreStable — filter_reason values used for excluded tiers come from the same enumerated set already in use (power_missing, etc.), not ad-hoc strings. Guards against drift across emitter sites.
4. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
5. Existing routing tests pass: service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests.
6. lefthook run pre-commit green.
